title: Craving for Alcohol & One Cheat Day  - CASE STUDY | HG Amitasana Das | HKM Mumbai
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

segments.text: I was hearing one lecture from Srila Prabhupada where he says, he was mentioning that let's say
when an army has to recruit soldiers, so where will they go to recruit soldiers and what kind
of people will they look for? Can anyone help me with an answer? Some people, well-built,
height, meeting certain height criteria, well-built, having that Kshatriya spirit, fighting spirit,
stamina, physical fitness. They will not go to some Vedantas where people are lean and thin
and they will not go to some Vedantas where people are lean and thin and they will not go to some
Vedantas where people are lean and thin and they will not go to some Vedantas where people are
and recruit soldiers from there. In other words, based on the requirement, you find an appropriate
person from a certain area where such kind of people are available. Now Krishna, as we all know,
this knowledge of Gita is eternal. It's not that at 5000 years back it was
spoken and before that it was non-existent. Krishna says, I spoke this knowledge to sun god
Vivasvan, Vivasvan spoke to Manu, Manu spoke to Kshwaku and in this way the knowledge was coming
down in parampara but somehow in due course of time the original message seemed to have got lost.
Therefore Arjuna, I am speaking this knowledge to you again and when was that? 5000 years back.
So in that sense knowledge of Gita is not 5000 year old knowledge.
Now Arjuna, Krishna wanted someone whom he can speak the Gita and this original knowledge
can be reinstated back, original understanding can be reinstated. Now whom should Krishna
look, who would be the right recipient of the message,
whom he can speak the Gita to?
Who will speak to this message, who will understand the message and will in turn pass it on.
So for army, I would look for soldiers who are well built.
If I have to deliver this message of Gita, what kind of people would I look for?
What kind of people? People who are scholarly, people who are brahmanas,
people who are having knowledge of Shastras, have a kind of inclination,
scholars, people who know Sanskrit, am I right?
But what did Krishna choose to receive this knowledge, whom did Krishna choose?
Arjuna, who is the Ksatria, a warrior, a family man, he was not even a brahmana, he was not a scholar,
he was not a pandit.
Only one bombed that.
chose that person to receive the message of Gita. Which means to say, to understand Gita,

I would like to learn Sanskrit. So I tend to put a question, I say, why do you want

the case.
Sagar, is the screen running? Is the text visible? Okay. Sagar was a frequent alcohol drinker, and he would drink on all
occasions, whether it meant celebrations or tragedy, including Mother's Day. When I was in the hostel, for
everything, there were people who used to drink. If they failed in exams, it was an occasion to drink.
And if they passed an exam, again, it was an occasion to drink. So whether it meant celebrations or tragedy, his only
connect with his friends was through drinking. After he started attending Gita Live sessions, he has stopped
drinking. He has now become an outcast in his friend circle, and now tagged as a boring person.
Sagar is quite sincere in his spiritual commitments, and managed to stay away from alcohol for the past 6 months.
However, he has occasional cravings for the drink, and tells himself that just one drink will do no harm.

So this purification is a life over life process. From where we end in one life, we take off from that point in next life. That's why we see people at different levels of intensity in following the principles of Krishna consciousness.

There are some people, for example, in my family, my brother, he's been for ages chanting one round. There is no inspiration or there is no motivation that I should increase it, I should go to the next level. He's happy with that.
And there could be somebody who is always eager. Okay, what's the next level? What's the next level? What's the next level? What can I do more? How can I fast track my purification?

So all of us are at different levels. So this level is also a reality. Let's bear in mind. There will be people stuck at this level. All I am saying is the danger or pitfall in this level of understanding.

A person will get tempted and he will make progress two steps, come back four steps. Make two step progress, come back four steps. He'll be like a pendulum going up and down, up and down, up and down.
Finally, at the end of 10 years, there's no tangible progress. And finally, he'll say, anyway, this process is not making much sense.
Because I am saying, I am not making much sense. I am not making much sense. I am not making much sense.
I am still the same person.

Guilt has a place. There's a healthy guilt. A person makes a mistake, he gets up, learns from the mistake and moves on.
That's different from a person saying he loses his self-esteem.
Every time we make a resolve and break it, we are withdrawing from our emotional bank balance, our self-esteem.

I have shared with you one very important thing which all of us will go through when we follow these kind of, you know, try to come out of our past habits.
The first stage is, anyone remembers?
We had talked about four stages.
First stage is called hopeless defeat.
We don't even know that we don't know.
We are in a hopeless state.
We are hopelessly getting defeated.
We are breaking the principles left, right, center.
That's the stage called hopeless defeat.
When we make a resolve that I will follow the principles, we move to a next stage called struggle and defeat.

We are struggling to follow, but because we are relatively new on this path and we are not fully aware of how temptations will strike us, we are not fully prepared, our vocabulary has not got built, we suddenly, you know, surprises strike up.
We are taken by surprise, temptation come and we are not prepared, we don't have that vocabulary, we fall down.
So, the next stage is called surprise.
Struggle and defeat.
We are struggling to win, but most of the time we end up getting defeated.
If a person perseveres, follows the process, starts experiencing a higher taste, he moves on to the next level.
What is that level?
Struggle and victory.
He is still struggling and most of the time he starts becoming victorious.
And if he still persists, perseveres, continues to follow the process, he reaches the final stage which is effortless victory.
